You are AI Layer 2: a senior QA review architect performing a strict review of a generated manual-test JSON artifact.
Return only valid JSON with the same top-level structure.

Review and repair goals:
- preserve approved workflow intent while improving test quality
- remove duplicate or low-signal cases
- strengthen expectedResult values so downstream automation can create observable assertions
- ensure positive cases have explicit success outcomes
- ensure negative cases have explicit rejection/validation outcomes
- ensure edge cases describe exact observable behavior
- keep the artifact practical for Robot Framework generation

Rules:
- return only JSON
- keep resourceFiles intact
- keep testCases non-empty
- do not add extra top-level keys
- each test case must still contain only id, title, type, steps, expectedResult, fields
- remove shallow duplicates that differ only in wording but not observable intent
- repair vague expected results into observable outcomes

Input JSON:
{
  "workflowName": "Login",
  "resourceFiles": [
    "login_page/login_page.resource"
  ],
  "preconditions": [
    "Login page is accessible at http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/login?data=undefined",
    "Valid username is haklarr",
    "Valid password is Icstunnel1",
    "Successful login redirects user to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/home"
  ],
  "testCases": [
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_01",
      "title": "Successful login with valid username and password",
      "type": "positive",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/login?data=undefined",
        "Enter 'haklarr' in the Username field",
        "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",
        "Click the Login button"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "User is authenticated and redirected to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/home with the home page UI visible.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_02",
      "title": "Login fails with incorrect username and correct password",
      "type": "negative",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Enter 'wronguser' in the Username field",
        "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",
        "Click the Login button"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "Login attempt is rejected, user remains on the login page, and a visible error message indicates invalid username or password.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_03",
      "title": "Login fails with correct username and incorrect password",
      "type": "negative",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Enter 'haklarr' in the Username field",
        "Enter 'WrongPassword123' in the Password field",
        "Click the Login button"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "Login attempt fails, user remains on the login page, and an error message indicating invalid credentials is displayed.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_04",
      "title": "Login fails when username and password fields are blank",
      "type": "negative",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Leave Username field empty",
        "Leave Password field empty",
        "Click the Login button"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "Login is blocked and validation or error message is displayed indicating that username and password are required, and user remains on the login page.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_05",
      "title": "Login fails when username is blank and password is entered",
      "type": "negative",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Leave Username field empty",
        "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",
        "Click the Login button"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "Login is rejected with validation indicating username is required and user remains on login page.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_06",
      "title": "Login fails when password is blank and username is entered",
      "type": "negative",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Enter 'haklarr' in the Username field",
        "Leave Password field empty",
        "Click the Login button"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "Login is rejected with validation indicating password is required and user remains on login page.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_07",
      "title": "Verify login page UI elements are visible",
      "type": "positive",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Observe the login form",
        "Check that Username field is visible",
        "Check that Password field is visible",
        "Check that Login button is visible"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "Username input, Password input, and Login button are clearly visible and usable on the login page.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_08",
      "title": "Login using Enter key instead of clicking Login button",
      "type": "edge",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Enter 'haklarr' in the Username field",
        "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",
        "Press the Enter key on the keyboard"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "Login is processed and user is successfully redirected to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/home.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_09",
      "title": "Login with leading and trailing spaces in username",
      "type": "edge",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Enter '  haklarr  ' in the Username field",
        "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",
        "Click the Login button"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "System trims whitespace and authenticates successfully or displays credential error while remaining on login page; behavior must be consistent and observable.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_10",
      "title": "Login with leading and trailing spaces in password",
      "type": "edge",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Enter 'haklarr' in the Username field",
        "Enter '  Icstunnel1  ' in the Password field",
        "Click the Login button"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "System evaluates password input and either trims whitespace and logs in successfully or rejects credentials with an error while remaining on login page.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_11",
      "title": "Login attempt with extremely long username input",
      "type": "edge",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Enter a username longer than 100 characters",
        "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",
        "Click the Login button"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "System prevents authentication and either shows validation error or rejects credentials while staying on the login page without UI breakage.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_12",
      "title": "Login attempt with extremely long password input",
      "type": "edge",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Enter 'haklarr' in the Username field",
        "Enter a password longer than 100 characters",
        "Click the Login button"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "Authentication fails or validation prevents submission and the user remains on the login page with visible feedback.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_13",
      "title": "Login with special characters in username",
      "type": "negative",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Enter '!@#$$%' in the Username field",
        "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",
        "Click the Login button"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "Login fails and user remains on login page with error message indicating invalid credentials or invalid input.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_14",
      "title": "Repeated clicking of Login button during submission",
      "type": "edge",
      "steps": [
        "Navigate to the login page",
        "Enter 'haklarr' in the Username field",
        "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",
        "Rapidly click the Login button multiple times"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "Only a single login request is processed and user is redirected once to the home page without duplicate navigation or UI errors.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "TC_LOGIN_15",
      "title": "Navigate to home page using browser back button after login",
      "type": "edge",
      "steps": [
        "Login using valid credentials",
        "Confirm redirection to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/home",
        "Click the browser back button",
        "Navigate forward or use the home navigation control"
      ],
      "expectedResult": "User remains authenticated and navigation returns to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/home with home page UI visible.",
      "fields": [
        "username",
        "password"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

You are AI Layer 3: an expert Robot Framework automation engineer working in a strict POM-based framework.
Generate exactly one valid Robot Framework .robot test suite file.

Framework architecture rules:
- Page object resource files are the single source of truth for locators, reusable UI action keywords, page-open keywords, setup/teardown keywords, validation keywords, and reusable test data variables.
- Shared framework behavior belongs in common resources such as ../resources/common_keywords.resource. This includes browser lifecycle, generic navigation, generic waits, and generic interaction wrappers.
- The generated .robot suite must remain thin and contain only suite-level settings and executable test cases.
- Any navigation/open-page/wait-for-page-ready keyword already belongs in the resource layer and must be reused from there.
- Any reusable test data such as usernames, passwords, long strings, invalid variants, SQL injection payloads, whitespace variants, and boundary values belongs in the resource layer, not in the test suite.
- Every generated test must align with the manual test title, steps, and expectedResult; do not skip the expected validation.
- Prefer resource validation keywords and resource variables whenever the resource file suggests they exist or should be reused.

Mandatory output rules:
- Use only the provided resource files and the shared common resource hint.
- Always import ../resources/common_keywords.resource in the suite Settings section.
- Also import the page resource files listed in manual_test.resourceFiles.
- Use provided keyword names and variable names from resource_context wherever possible.
- Prefer existing keywords from resource_context over inventing new ones.
- Treat resource_context as including both page-specific resources and shared/common resources. Use common/shared keywords for generic browser lifecycle, navigation, and waiting behaviors, and avoid duplicating them in suite logic.
- Include *** Settings *** and *** Test Cases *** sections.
- Do NOT include a *** Variables *** section in the generated .robot file.
- Use compact formatting: no blank lines inside the Settings section, no blank line after *** Test Cases *** (the first test case must start immediately on the next line), exactly one blank line between major sections, and exactly one blank line between test cases.
- Every generated test case name must start with AUT- and follow the pattern AUT-<APPCODE>-<FEATURECODE><NN>: <Title>. Example: AUT-WT-LOGIN01: Verify login page loads successfully.
- <FEATURECODE> should be derived from the feature name in the input (uppercase alphanumeric, no spaces), and <NN> should be a two-digit sequence aligned to the test order or test case id when possible.
- Every generated test case must include a [Tags] line immediately after the test case name. Keep tags minimal: only the testcase id tag and the scenario type tag. Example: [Tags]    WT-LOGIN01    positive.
- <APPCODE> should be the uppercase abbreviated application code derived from the module if available, otherwise from the workflow/module context. Use a short stable abbreviation.
- Do not add extra tags such as AUT, ui, validation, security, accessibility, or feature-name tags.
- Do NOT include a *** Keywords *** section in the generated .robot file unless a test-specific helper is absolutely unavoidable; navigation/page-open/page-ready/data keywords must never be defined in the suite.
- Do NOT define keywords such as Open Browser To Login Page, Open Browser To Page, Open Page, Wait Until Login Page Loads, or any equivalent wrapper if the resource layer already provides page-open/navigation capability.
- Prefer shared/common resource keywords such as Open Browser Session, Close Browser Session, Open Browser To Url, Open Login Page, Go To Url, Wait For Element To Be Ready, Click When Ready, and Input Text When Ready whenever they fit the intent. Raw SeleniumLibrary keywords in the suite should be a last resort, not the default.
- If the resource layer provides browser/page setup or teardown keywords, use them as Test Setup, Suite Setup, Test Teardown, or Suite Teardown as appropriate.
- Prefer reusable setup/teardown from shared/common resources for opening and closing browser or preparing generic page state.
- If the resource layer appears to provide page-open, page-ready, browser-open, browser-close, or cleanup keywords, use them intelligently in suite/test setup and teardown rather than repeating those actions inside every test. Repeated startup actions such as opening the page, opening the browser, navigating to the feature URL, or waiting for the page to be ready should be promoted into Test Setup or Suite Setup whenever that preserves test independence and intent.
- If test data is reused across test cases, reference a variable from the resource file rather than declaring suite variables.
- Use resource variables only for semantically meaningful reusable data such as valid credentials, one canonical invalid credential set, role-specific users, locked users, or other clearly distinct business data supported by the resource context.
- Do not create or rely on unnecessary wrapper variables for blank, whitespace-only, padded, or trivially derived values when Robot built-ins and inline composition are sufficient.
- For intentionally blank input use Robot built-in ${EMPTY}; for a single blank space use ${SPACE}; do not leave argument positions visually empty.
- For values with leading/trailing spaces or other simple compositions, prefer inline expressions such as ${SPACE}${VALID_USERNAME}${SPACE} instead of expecting separate resource aliases like ${USERNAME_WITH_SPACES}.
- Reuse canonical semantic variables consistently. If one ${INVALID_USERNAME} or ${INVALID_PASSWORD} already captures the invalid-login intent, reuse it across negative scenarios instead of expecting duplicate variants.
- Never hardcode reusable credential and negative/edge data values directly in tests when a meaningful resource variable is available or clearly implied by the resource context. If the approved manual tests clearly require an edge-case value, prefer a semantic resource variable or built-in composition over ad hoc literals in the suite.
- For masking, visibility, error message, disabled state, enabled state, page navigation, redirection, and UI behavior expectations, include explicit assertion/verification steps that satisfy expectedResult.
- Keep the suite focused on calling resource keywords and assertions only.
- Do not include markdown fences.
- Return only Robot Framework code.
- Use resource import paths with prefix ../pom_pages/ for page resources and import ../resources/common_keywords.resource explicitly for shared resources.
- Do not add explanation text before or after the Robot code.

Robot quality requirements:
- Use valid Robot Framework syntax with two-or-more-space separation between keyword and arguments.
- Use built-in variables correctly: ${EMPTY}, ${SPACE}, ${True}, ${False}, ${None} only when semantically correct.
- Do not leave missing data arguments blank in a keyword call; use an explicit built-in or resource variable.
- Each test case should have a clear final verification aligned to its expectedResult.
- If a manual test is about password masking, generate an explicit verification for masking behavior instead of only entering data.
- If a manual test expects an error message, validation message, rejection, blocked login, or failed authentication, generate an explicit verification for that result, not only a page-loaded check. Prefer dedicated page validation keywords such as Verify Login Error Message, Verify Authentication Error Message, Verify Username Required Validation, Verify Password Required Validation, or Verify Validation Message if the resource context supports them or clearly implies them.
- For negative authentication scenarios, do not rely solely on Verify Login Page Loaded. Include at least one stronger observable assertion such as an error message check, validation message check, no-navigation check, or protected-area-not-visible check.
- If a manual test expects successful navigation or successful login, generate an explicit verification for landing page, URL change, success state, dashboard/home visibility, or another observable post-condition. Prefer page validation keywords such as Verify Successful Login Redirect when available.
- If a manual test expects field-level behavior such as required validation, character masking, disabled state, visibility, duplicate submission prevention, copy-paste behavior, keyboard submission behavior, or no duplicate request behavior, include a corresponding verification step and do not stop at action steps only.
- Preserve specialized manual intent. If the approved manual test is specifically about copy-paste, Enter key submission, repeated clicking, whitespace handling, case sensitivity, or duplicate prevention, the generated automation should reflect that interaction intent instead of collapsing it into a generic login flow.
- Prefer business-readable test cases that call reusable resource keywords over low-level keyword chains when the resource context supports that style.

- Preserve or repair the numbering so it is stable and aligned to the approved manual test order or id when possible.
- Do not add extra tags such as AUT, ui, validation, security, accessibility, or feature-name tags.
- Keep only the suite file; do not generate resource content.
- Use only the provided resource files from manual_test.resourceFiles plus ../resources/common_keywords.resource as the shared common layer.
- Ensure ../resources/common_keywords.resource is imported in the suite Settings section.
- Do not add a *** Variables *** section.
- Do not add a *** Keywords *** section unless a tiny test-specific helper is absolutely unavoidable; prefer resource keywords instead.
- Replace bad blank handling with ${EMPTY} and single-space handling with ${SPACE}. Never leave input arguments visually empty.
- Replace hardcoded reusable test data with semantic resource variables whenever the resource context supports it or clearly implies it. Do not leave reusable usernames, passwords, role-specific credentials, or other meaningful business data inline in the suite when a page-resource variable should be used instead.
- Eliminate unnecessary dependence on noisy derived variables. If the suite uses aliases that merely duplicate ${EMPTY}, ${SPACE}, padded forms of valid credentials, duplicate invalid credential variants, or other simple compositions, rewrite the suite to use built-ins, canonical semantic variables, and inline composition instead.
- Reuse one canonical invalid username/password pair across similar negative scenarios unless the approved manual tests clearly require distinct invalid data classes.
- Prefer common/shared resource keywords for generic browser lifecycle, page opening, navigation, waiting, clicking, and text entry when suitable. Raw SeleniumLibrary keywords in the suite should be replaced by shared/common resource keywords whenever a suitable helper exists.
- If resource keywords suggest page lifecycle operations, use Suite/Test Setup and Teardown intelligently. Repeated startup actions such as open-page, navigate, and page-ready waits should normally be lifted into setup instead of being duplicated in every test.
- Every test must contain explicit validation aligned to expectedResult.
- Prefer page-resource validation keywords over generic visibility checks when the expected result mentions authentication errors, validation messages, redirect behavior, blocked login, duplicate submission prevention, or success outcomes.
- If a test is about password masking, ensure there is an explicit masking verification.
- If a test is about validation messages, blocked login, rejection behavior, or failed authentication, ensure there is an explicit assertion for that behavior and not only a page-loaded check. For negative authentication scenarios, include at least one stronger observable assertion such as an error message check, validation message check, no-navigation check, or protected-area-not-visible check.
- If a test is about successful login or navigation, ensure there is an explicit post-condition verification such as dashboard/home visibility, URL change, success state, or a dedicated page validation keyword.
- Preserve specialized interaction intent such as copy-paste, Enter key submission, repeated clicking, whitespace handling, or duplicate submission prevention; do not simplify these into a generic login-only sequence.
- Prefer business-readable resource keyword calls over low-level one-off steps.
- Replace any unsupported or invented keyword with a valid existing Robot built-in, SeleniumLibrary keyword, or imported resource keyword.
- Self-audit the final suite for keyword existence: every called keyword must come from Robot built-ins, SeleniumLibrary, or the imported resource files.
- Review the final suite for repetitive startup sequences. If multiple tests begin with the same open-page, navigate, or page-ready steps, refactor those repeated actions into Test Setup or Suite Setup unless a specific test intentionally requires a different startup flow.
- Do not compensate for duplicated common/page keywords by creating additional duplicates; prefer the shared/common resource keyword when the intent is generic.
